prompt: I'm adding some jQuery to my OA control's JavaScript file. I wrapped my initialization code in $(document).ready() but it doesn't seem to fire at all. What's the correct way to wire up JavaScript in an OA control?

assertions.name: explains-why-ready-fails
assertions.description: Explains that $(document).ready fails because the OA framework loads jQuery after the script files execute, so jQuery isn't available at script parse time

assertions.name: recommends-sys-application-add-load
assertions.description: Recommends Sys.Application.add_load as the correct OA replacement for $(document).ready

assertions.name: warns-iife-also-fails
assertions.description: Mentions that a jQuery IIFE wrapper (e.g. (function($){...})(jQuery)) also fails for the same reason — not just the document.ready form

assertions.name: scriptmanager-for-codebehind
assertions.description: If mentioning code-behind script injection, recommends ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock rather than Page.ClientScript (which is silently ignored when ScriptManager is present)
